description: Self-contained stereochemistry benchmark with 80 positive transformation cases: 40 original reaction-SMILES cases, four reviewed non-tetrahedral/atrop rewrite fixtures, seven reviewed Phase 2R electron-flow × stereo fixtures, 21 reviewed native descriptor rewrites promoted from the July 2026 candidate pool, and eight reviewed final-eight native descriptor rewrites. Eight negative assertions remain corruption/specification fixtures and do not count toward the positive baseline.

notes: local_geometry, descriptor_change and product_population are three independent axes. ST-10 is retained/flipped, ST-03 is inverted/retained, and ST-20 is retained/destroyed; no single verdict per reaction is sufficient.

Never compare @ against @@ across the arrow. Both symbols depend on SMILES neighbour order. Use the mapped-neighbour permutation parity of Section 10, then recompute CIP independently on each side. ST-22 exists to catch exactly this bug.

expected_unique_stereoisomer_count counts distinct product stereoisomers after atom maps are cleared. A fully specified template contributes 1; a count of 2 must come from an explicit RACEMIC entry in rule_metadata.stereo_outcomes, never from the SMILES.
'racemic' asserts a 50:50 composition; 'unknown' asserts that the composition was not determined. They are different claims and must not compare equal. ST-45 and ST-46 test the distinction.

step_relationship alternative_face_rules means the steps are correlated alternatives whose union is the oracle. Branching each stereocentre independently would additionally generate the meso combinations and is wrong; see ST-04 and ST-07.
For multi-step cases the oracle describes the composed sequence, not any single step. ST-11's two inversions compose to net retention.
graph_only means SynKit preserves the declared product descriptor in the ITS/product graph but the current reaction-SMILES export does not preserve it end to end.

Parsing note, verified against RDKit 2026.03.1: Chem.MolFromSmiles silently discards mapped explicit hydrogens that are bound to a heavy atom, so [C@:2]([H:6])(...) loses map 6 while the free [H:6][H:7] survives. Consumers must parse the mapped_reaction fields with SmilesParserParams(removeHs=False), otherwise map conservation appears to fail for every case that tracks a transferred hydrogen (ST-14 through ST-16, ST-19, ST-20, ST-30 through ST-32, ST-37).
native_stereo_rewrite cases carry authoritative endpoint descriptors and StereoChange sidecars. Their reaction SMILES supplies Lewis-state connectivity; it is not itself the stereo oracle.

cases.name: SN1 solvolysis of (R)-1-phenylethyl chloride

oracle.notes: Substrate configuration fixed to R to agree with the catalog annotation for ST-01/TH-07. The single capture template serialises one enantiomer; the equal pair comes from rule_metadata, not from the SMILES.

cases.name: Ordinary SN2 inversion of (R)-2-bromobutane
SN2 inversion with unchanged R CIP label

oracle.notes: Walden inversion and the Br-to-CN priority reordering cancel at the CIP-label level.

cases.name: Syn deuteration of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Corrected: the correlated R,R/S,S pair needs two deterministic face rules, exactly as in ST-07. A single fully specified template emits one enantiomer only, and independent per-centre branching would also generate the incorrect meso combinations.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentres.

cases.name: Anti bromination of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Single rule suffices: the two face matches give enantiomeric assignments of a meso product, i.e. the same compound.

cases.name: Syn deuteration of Z-but-2-ene

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentres.

cases.name: Anti bromination of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: The correlated R,R/S,S pair is represented by two deterministic face rules; independent per-centre branching would also generate incorrect meso combinations.

cases.name: Fischer esterification of (S)-lactic acid at the carboxyl group

oracle.notes: No bond to the stereocentre is touched; acyl-oxygen cleavage keeps both geometry and label.

cases.name: Remote SN2 that does not change CIP priorities at the stereocentre

oracle.notes: Reaction is two bonds away; the OH > CH2CH2X > CH3 > H ranking survives Br -> CN.

cases.name: Remote substitution that reverses CIP priorities while geometry is retained

oracle.notes: The catalog's formal TH-03 example. CH2Br > CH2Cl becomes CH2Cl > CH2F, so ranks 2 and 3 swap and the label flips although no bond to the centre is broken. A system that infers geometry from the CIP label alone fails here.

cases.name: Two consecutive SN2 reactions at the same centre give net retention

oracle.notes: Oracle is over the composed two-step sequence: inverted then inverted is net retention. Each individual step is inverted; a per-step checker must not collapse the pair into a single retention edit.

cases.name: Enolate-mediated racemization of (S)-2-methylbutanal

oracle.notes: Connectivity is unchanged across the pair; only the stereo element is destroyed and rebuilt. Structurally parallel to ST-01 but with no leaving group, so a checker keyed on connectivity change must still see the stereo edit.

cases.name: Hypochlorite oxidation of (R)-2-butanol to 2-butanone

oracle.notes: Both enantiomers are accepted and converge on the same achiral ketone, so rejected_substrates is deliberately empty.

cases.name: Achiral reduction of 2-butanone creates a racemic centre

oracle.notes: Same graph edit as ST-15/ST-16; only the rule metadata differs. The template serialises one enantiomer, which is not the oracle.

cases.name: Asymmetric reduction of 2-butanone to (R)-2-butanol

oracle.notes: Identical graph edit to ST-14 and ST-16; the facial choice lives in the rule, not in the connectivity.

cases.name: Asymmetric reduction of 2-butanone to (S)-2-butanol

oracle.notes: Enantiomeric catalyst to ST-15; the two cases must not be deduplicated to one rule.

cases.name: Homolysis at a stereogenic carbon flattens the centre

oracle.notes: The sec-butyl radical is treated as planar. Pairs with ST-18; see also NG-06 in the catalog for the cage-model caveat.

cases.name: Radical recombination in an achiral environment recreates a racemate
Enzymatic deuteride delivery creates an isotope-only stereocentre

oracle.notes: (R)-[1-2H]ethanol. C2 carries CH3, OH, H and D, so the centre exists only by virtue of the isotope: the CIP decision falls to Rule 2 (mass number) after Rule 1 ties.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentre.

cases.name: Making two substituents identical destroys an intact stereocentre

oracle.notes: The hard case for the local_geometry/descriptor split: no bond to C2 is touched, so the local geometry is retained, yet the centre stops being stereogenic because CH2Cl becomes a second CH3. Propan-2-ol is achiral. Systems that equate 'centre destroyed' with 'bond to centre broken' fail here.

cases.name: Azide displacement at one of two stereocentres

oracle.notes: local_geometry/descriptor_change in this oracle refer to the reacting centre atom:4. The spectator centre atom:2 is retained at R and must be reported unchanged; per-molecule diffing that flags 'stereochemistry changed' without localising it fails this case.

cases.name: Exchanged mapping of two equivalent methyls must not report a false inversion

oracle.notes: The two variants are the same reaction; maps 1 and 3 (the two isopropyl methyls) are exchanged in the product of variant_b. Both must yield exactly one identical product and report inverted at atom:5 only. Reporting any change at atom:2 is a false positive.

cases.name: Syn dihydroxylation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Isotope-free analogue of ST-04: same geometry, executable today. AD-03 and ST-04 must agree on the E + syn -> chiral pair outcome even though D is CIP rank 3 while OH is rank 1.

cases.name: Syn dihydroxylation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Isotope-free analogue of ST-06.

cases.name: Epoxidation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: trans-2,3-dimethyloxirane is chiral, so the achiral peracid gives a racemate. The oxygen is delivered suprafacially, hence relative_stereochemistry syn even though the catalog table calls the product 'trans'.

cases.name: Epoxidation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: cis-2,3-dimethyloxirane is meso.

cases.name: Cyclopropanation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Singlet carbene adds suprafacially; trans-1,2-dimethylcyclopropane is chiral.

cases.name: Cyclopropanation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: cis-1,2-dimethylcyclopropane is meso.

cases.name: HBr addition to 1-butene via a planar secondary cation

oracle.notes: Markovnikov regiochemistry plus a racemic centre. The alkene has no E/Z element to destroy, so this isolates centre creation from descriptor destruction.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of 2-phenyl-1-butene with an achiral catalyst

oracle.notes: Prochiral alkene, achiral catalyst: both enantiotopic faces are used. Same graph edit as ST-31.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of 2-phenyl-1-butene with a chiral catalyst

oracle.notes: Identical connectivity change to ST-30; only the facial selectivity annotation differs.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of E-but-2-ene leaves no stereochemical signal

oracle.notes: The E/Z element is destroyed and butane carries no stereo element. Z-but-2-ene is deliberately NOT rejected: it is a legitimate substrate giving the same product, which is what makes this addition non-stereospecific in the observable sense. Contrast ST-04, where the same syn delivery is stereospecific only because the isotope label survives.

cases.name: Remote coupling reverses alkene CIP priority while geometry is retained

oracle.notes: Double-bond analogue of ST-10. On C4 the ranking CH2Br > n-propyl becomes n-propyl > ethyl, so the reference substituent moves to the other side of the double bond and the label flips E -> Z with the geometry untouched.

known_blocker.observed_effect: The product graph retains the planar descriptor, but generated reaction SMILES omits the expected Z descriptor.

cases.name: Photochemical E to Z isomerization of but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Connectivity is completely unchanged; the whole edit is the stereo element. A rule engine keyed on bond changes alone produces an empty ITS here.

cases.name: Z to E isomerization of but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Connectivity is completely unchanged; the whole edit is the stereo element. A rule engine keyed on bond changes alone produces an empty ITS here.

cases.name: Reductive ozonolysis of E-but-2-ene destroys the double-bond stereo

oracle.notes: Z-but-2-ene gives the identical product; the stereo element is destroyed by C=C cleavage rather than by saturation, unlike ST-32.

cases.name: Lindlar semihydrogenation of but-2-yne creates the Z-alkene

oracle.notes: Syn delivery of both hydrogens on the catalyst surface. Same graph edit as ST-38; the geometry comes only from the condition annotation.

cases.name: Dissolving-metal reduction of but-2-yne creates the E-alkene

oracle.notes: Pairs with ST-37: identical connectivity change, opposite geometry, decided by the trans-vinyl-anion intermediate. The two cases must not deduplicate to one rule.

known_blocker.observed_effect: explicit_h=true is required to generate the product; the product graph contains E but generated reaction SMILES is stereo-free.

cases.name: Zinc debromination of meso-2,3-dibromobutane gives E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Exact microscopic reverse of ST-05. Two tetrahedral centres are destroyed and one E/Z element is created in a single edit, so local_geometry and descriptor_change refer to different loci: this is the case that breaks any single-valued stereo verdict per reaction.

known_blocker.observed_effect: The product graph retains E, but generated reaction SMILES contains an unspecified double bond.

cases.name: Zinc debromination of RR/SS-2,3-dibromobutane gives Z-but-2-ene

rule_set_metadata.note: Two enantiomeric substrates converge on one achiral product.

oracle.notes: Reverse of ST-07 and the mirror-image test of ST-39. Both enantiomers of the substrate are needed and both give Z; a rule set that keeps only one enantiomer silently halves the substrate scope.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Both product graphs retain Z, but generated reaction SMILES contains an unspecified double bond.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit square-planar serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied geometric isomerization only; no pathway or energetic feasibility is asserted.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit TB serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: The endpoint inversion is supplied. No Berry-pseudorotation path, barrier, or preferred geometry is inferred.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit OH serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: The endpoint inversion is supplied. No twist mechanism, barrier, or preferred geometry is inferred.

cases.event: ortho-substituted biaryl radical recombination with declared racemic axial outcome
cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit assigned-atrop serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor branching.
cases.claim_boundary: The rule declares an assigned axial population. Connectivity alone is not used to infer rotational stability, barrier, or atropisomerism.

cases.name: remote tetrahedral descriptor is preserved during polar capture
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: SN2 electron flow inverts the supplied tetrahedral descriptor
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: radical homolysis breaks the supplied tetrahedral descriptor
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: reverse radical homolysis forms the supplied tetrahedral descriptor
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: elimination electron flow forms the supplied planar-bond descriptor
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: reverse elimination breaks the supplied planar-bond descriptor
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: polar capture records an explicitly unknown tetrahedral outcome
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied typed descriptor is checked against declared electron flow; the fixture does not infer stereochemical selectivity or 3D geometry.

cases.name: trigonal-bipyramidal stereoisomerization (A)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: the five ligands are pairwise constitutionally distinct, so a single transposition lies outside the descriptor stabiliser and genuinely flips the +/-1 parity rather than being a degenerate exchange of equivalent ligands. Reversibility: applying the rule twice restores the original parity.
cases.chemical_rationale: Neutral phosphorane P(V) with five distinct ligands (F, Cl, Br, CH3, H); the centre is therefore stereogenic and the two parities are real, distinct stereoisomers.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests INVERT of a trigonal-bipyramidal relative descriptor under an odd ligand permutation. Connectivity is identical on both sides because this is a pure stereoisomerization: the descriptor sidecar carries the whole change. Infers no pseudorotation barrier or mechanism.

cases.name: atropisomeric biaryl axis formation (single specified axis)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Lifecycle correctness: the axis descriptor has no reactant counterpart (before null); its four quadrant references (10,14 on ring A; 15,19 on ring B) exist only once the 2-4 bond is formed. Target ownership uses the smaller-larger bond key convention. Reverse is the BREAK of the axis.
cases.chemical_rationale: Formal Suzuki-type coupling written charge-balanced with explicit boron/halide bookkeeping. Each ring carries one differentiating ortho substituent (Br, Cl) and one ortho H, so all four quadrants are distinguished and the axis is stereogenic.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests FORM of a supplied atrop-axis relative descriptor across a new biaryl bond, with a single specified axis outcome (deliberately distinct from the existing atrop-formation-with-RACEMIC fixture). Infers no rotational barrier, atropisomer stability or coupling mechanism.

cases.name: phosphorus stereocentre inversion at P(=O) (0)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: the four P ligands (CH3, aryl, oxo, X) are constitutionally distinct so the centre is stereogenic; the leaving and incoming ligands share one descriptor slot and the parity flip is a genuine inversion. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: A neutral P(V) oxide bearing four distinct ligands is configurationally stable. Charge-balanced substitution with explicit anion bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests INVERT at a configurationally stable four-coordinate P(=O) stereocentre. Infers no substitution mechanism, no pentacoordinate intermediate and no rate.

cases.name: silicon stereocentre retention (0)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: four constitutionally distinct ligands (Me, aryl, Et, X) make Si stereogenic; the incoming ligand takes the leaving ligand's descriptor slot with unchanged parity, encoding retention. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Neutral Si(IV) with four distinct ligands; charge-balanced substitution with explicit anion bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE at a four-coordinate silicon stereocentre under substitution with configurational retention. The supplied before/after descriptors are the oracle; no mechanism, rate or retention/inversion preference is inferred.

cases.name: quaternary ammonium retained (Br)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: four constitutionally distinct alkyl ligands make N(+) stereogenic; none is permuted or replaced by the remote substitution, so parity is preserved. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Quaternary N(+) (methyl, ethyl, CH2X, propyl); the C-Br bond two atoms away is substituted. Cationic centre with explicit counter-anion exchange keeps charge balance.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE at a configurationally stable quaternary ammonium centre. Explicitly does NOT treat an ordinary rapidly inverting trivalent amine as configurationally stable: the centre here is a four-ligand N(+) with no lone pair and hence no pyramidal inversion pathway.

cases.name: sulfide oxidation forms @LP sulfoxide stereocentre

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Lifecycle correctness: the sulfide sulfur bears two lone pairs and two identical-role references, so it owns no specified descriptor (before null); the sulfoxide sulfur has three real ligands plus exactly one stereochemically active lone pair, giving a well-defined four-reference tetrahedral descriptor with @LP:1 owned by centre 1. Reverse is the BREAK reduction.
cases.chemical_rationale: Oxidation of a dialkyl sulfide to the sulfoxide by hydrogen peroxide, written atom- and charge-balanced with water as the by-product.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests FORM of a tetrahedral descriptor whose fourth reference is the typed virtual reference @LP:1: a sulfide S is not a stereocentre, the derived sulfoxide S is. Infers no oxidant selectivity, mechanism or rate.

cases.name: prochiral ketone reduction to unequal enantiomer mixture (0.7)

oracle.notes: The constitutional edit matches ST-14 through ST-16, but the declared 0.7/0.3 ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE is a fourth, intentionally distinct rule outcome: it is neither racemic nor a single stereoisomer.

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Outcome separation: the descriptor lifecycle is FORM while the population is ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE; the two are independent and the mixture does NOT substitute for UNSPECIFIED (both branches are explicitly enumerated with definite parities). Map-independent product counting: the two branches are inverse relative descriptors that remain distinct after atom maps are cleared, so expected_unique_stereoisomer_count is 2. Determinacy is not_stereospecific, consistent with a branching outcome.
cases.chemical_rationale: Reduction of butan-2-one gives butan-2-ol, whose C2 (map 1) carries four constitutionally distinct ligands (methyl, hydroxyl, ethyl, H) and is therefore genuinely stereogenic; the two parities are true enantiomers. Atom- and charge-balanced.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests an ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE population at a newly formed atom stereocentre: two inverse branches with unequal weights [0.7, 0.3] summing to 1. THE WEIGHTS ARE SUPPLIED SOFTWARE-TEST PARAMETERS, NOT a measured or predicted enantiomeric excess; no experimental selectivity, catalyst, reagent control or rate is claimed or implied.

cases.name: biaryl coupling to unequal atropisomer mixture (0.55, Br/F)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: an atrop_bond carries +/-1 parity, so its two branches are genuine ENANTIOMERS (aR/aS) and the ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE kind is appropriate for a bond locus. A planar_bond would be wrong here: its parity is 0 and its two geometries are diastereomers, not enantiomers. Outcome separation: lifecycle is FORM while population is a two-branch mixture; the branches remain distinct after clearing atom maps, so expected_unique_stereoisomer_count is 2.
cases.chemical_rationale: Formal Suzuki-type biaryl coupling in which each ring bears one differentiating ortho substituent and one ortho H, so all four quadrants are distinguished and the newly formed axis is stereogenic. Charge-balanced with explicit boron/halide bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests an ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE population at a newly formed BOND locus: the two branches are the two senses of an atropisomeric axis, with unequal weights [0.55, 0.45] summing to 1. THE WEIGHTS ARE SUPPLIED SOFTWARE-TEST PARAMETERS, NOT a measured or predicted atroposelectivity; no experimental selectivity, rotational barrier or atropisomer stability is claimed.

cases.name: meso diol formation (two centres, single achiral product)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Target ownership: each formed centre owns its own key (atom:2, atom:4) and its own FORM effect. Permutation correctness: the two descriptors use analogous reference orderings (alkyl, hydroxyl, partner carbon, H) with OPPOSITE parity, which is exactly the internal-mirror relationship; because the two centres are constitutionally equivalent, that opposite-parity pair is achiral overall and counts as ONE stereoisomer after atom maps are cleared, so expected_unique_stereoisomer_count is 1 and the population is a single stereoisomer, not an enantiomeric mixture. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Double reduction of butane-2,3-dione gives butane-2,3-diol; the (R,S) combination is the meso diastereomer, which is achiral by virtue of its internal mirror plane despite containing two stereocentres. Atom- and charge-balanced with explicit hydrogens.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests LOCALIZED multicentre behaviour of a different kind: two stereocentres are FORMED in one rewrite with a meso (internal-mirror) relationship, so the population is a SINGLE achiral stereoisomer rather than a mixture. Infers no diastereoselectivity, reagent control or rate; the meso outcome is the supplied oracle, not a prediction.

cases.name: octahedral break on ligand loss

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Lifecycle correctness: the six-ligand locus has no product counterpart (after null). Reverse is the FORM association.
cases.chemical_rationale: Bromide departs from an anionic six-coordinate cobalt, giving a neutral five-coordinate centre; charge is balanced (-1 -> 0 + -1).
cases.claim_boundary: Tests BREAK of an octahedral descriptor when a donor dissociates; infers no product geometry or energetics.

cases.name: octahedral formation on ligand association

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Lifecycle correctness: the six-ligand locus exists only in the product (before null). Reverse is the BREAK dissociation.
cases.chemical_rationale: Iodide associates with a five-coordinate centre giving an anionic six-coordinate metal; charge tracked.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests FORM of an octahedral descriptor on donor association; infers no geometry.

cases.name: atropisomeric axis inversion (A)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: the four ortho references are pairwise distinguished (Br/H on ring A, Cl/H on ring B), so the two axial senses are genuinely distinct and the +/-1 parity flip is not degenerate. Reversible by double application.
cases.chemical_rationale: Axially chiral biaryl with one differentiating ortho substituent per ring; the two parities are the aR/aS atropisomers.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests INVERT of a supplied atrop-axis descriptor. Connectivity is identical on both sides because atropisomerization is a pure stereoisomerization. Infers no rotational barrier or interconversion rate.

cases.name: atrop axis preserved under remote ortho edit

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: the six axis references (10,14,2,4,15,19) are unchanged and ring B remains quadrant-differentiated, so the parity is preserved. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Cl -> F exchange at the ring-B ortho position keeps the axis stereogenic; the descriptor references the ring atom (map 15), not its substituent. Charge tracked.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE of a supplied atrop-axis descriptor when an ortho substituent is exchanged for another differentiating group; infers no barrier or stability.

cases.name: phosphorus stereocentre inversion at P(=O) (2)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: the four P ligands (CH3, aryl, oxo, X) are constitutionally distinct so the centre is stereogenic; the leaving and incoming ligands share one descriptor slot and the parity flip is a genuine inversion. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: A neutral P(V) oxide bearing four distinct ligands is configurationally stable. Charge-balanced substitution with explicit anion bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests INVERT at a configurationally stable four-coordinate P(=O) stereocentre. Infers no substitution mechanism, no pentacoordinate intermediate and no rate.

cases.name: silicon stereocentre retention (1)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: four constitutionally distinct ligands (Me, aryl, Et, X) make Si stereogenic; the incoming ligand takes the leaving ligand's descriptor slot with unchanged parity, encoding retention. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Neutral Si(IV) with four distinct ligands; charge-balanced substitution with explicit anion bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE at a four-coordinate silicon stereocentre under substitution with configurational retention. The supplied before/after descriptors are the oracle; no mechanism, rate or retention/inversion preference is inferred.

cases.name: quaternary ammonium retained (I)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: four constitutionally distinct alkyl ligands make N(+) stereogenic; none is permuted or replaced by the remote substitution, so parity is preserved. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Quaternary N(+) (methyl, ethyl, CH2X, propyl); the C-I bond two atoms away is substituted. Cationic centre with explicit counter-anion exchange keeps charge balance.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE at a configurationally stable quaternary ammonium centre. Explicitly does NOT treat an ordinary rapidly inverting trivalent amine as configurationally stable: the centre here is a four-ligand N(+) with no lone pair and hence no pyramidal inversion pathway.

cases.name: sulfoxide oxidation to sulfone breaks @LP stereocentre

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Lifecycle correctness: the sulfoxide descriptor's fourth reference @LP:1 is consumed by the incoming oxo ligand, and the product sulfur carries two constitutionally equivalent oxo ligands, so no valid stereogenic descriptor survives (after null). Reverse is the FORM reduction.
cases.chemical_rationale: Oxidation of a sulfoxide to the sulfone by hydrogen peroxide, atom- and charge-balanced with water as the by-product. The two S=O ligands are equivalent, which is why the locus disappears rather than merely changing parity.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests BREAK of an @LP-referenced tetrahedral descriptor: oxidation replaces the stereochemically active lone pair with a second oxo ligand, and the resulting sulfone sulfur is not stereogenic. Infers no oxidant selectivity or rate.

cases.name: phosphine alkylation: @LP reference replaced with retention

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: both endpoints key on atom:1, but the reactant's fourth reference is @LP:1 and the product's is the new C ligand (map 5). Permutation correctness: the incoming ligand occupies the lone pair's descriptor slot without permuting the other three references, so equal parity encodes retention. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: A trisubstituted phosphine (unlike a trivalent amine) is treated as configurationally stable; its lone pair is @LP:1. Alkylation gives a four-ligand phosphonium; charge tracked with explicit iodide.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests PRESERVE across a change of reference KIND: the fourth reference migrates from the typed virtual @LP:1 to a real ligand map while the relative configuration is retained. Asserts no mechanism, rate or inversion barrier; phosphine configurational stability is a modelling premise.

cases.name: acetophenone reduction to unequal enantiomer mixture (0.8)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Outcome separation: lifecycle is FORM while population is ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE; the mixture does NOT substitute for UNSPECIFIED since both branches carry definite inverse parities. Map-independent counting: the branches stay distinct after clearing maps, so the count is 2. Determinacy is not_stereospecific, consistent with branching.
cases.chemical_rationale: Reduction of acetophenone gives a carbinol whose stereocentre carries four constitutionally distinct ligands, so the two parities are true enantiomers. Atom- and charge-balanced.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests an ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE population at a newly formed atom stereocentre: two inverse branches with unequal weights [0.8, 0.2] summing to 1. THE WEIGHTS ARE SUPPLIED SOFTWARE-TEST PARAMETERS, NOT a measured or predicted enantiomeric excess; no selectivity, catalyst control or rate is claimed.

cases.name: biaryl coupling to unequal atropisomer mixture (0.62, I/Cl)

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Permutation correctness: atrop_bond carries +/-1 parity so its two branches are genuine ENANTIOMERS (aR/aS), making ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE correct for a bond locus; planar_bond would be wrong since its parity is 0 and its geometries are diastereomers. Branches stay distinct after clearing maps, so the count is 2.
cases.chemical_rationale: Formal Suzuki-type coupling; each ring bears one differentiating ortho substituent and one ortho H, so all four quadrants are distinguished and the new axis is stereogenic. Charge-balanced with explicit boron/halide bookkeeping.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests an ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE at a newly formed BOND locus with unequal weights [0.62, 0.38]. THE WEIGHTS ARE SUPPLIED SOFTWARE-TEST PARAMETERS, NOT a measured atroposelectivity; no rotational barrier or atropisomer stability is claimed.

cases.name: meso diol formation, pentane-2,4-diol variant

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo-candidate pool generator, v2

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative ligand references, so a consistent atom-map renaming leaves it equivalent. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre/central bond and matches every endpoint and effect key. Target ownership: each formed centre owns its key (atom:2, atom:4) and its own FORM effect. Permutation correctness: the two descriptors use analogous reference orderings with OPPOSITE parity, the internal-mirror relationship; because the centres are constitutionally equivalent, that pair is achiral overall and counts as ONE stereoisomer after clearing maps, so the count is 1 and the population is single, not an enantiomeric mixture. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: Double reduction of pentane-2,4-dione gives pentane-2,4-diol, whose (R,S) combination is the achiral meso diastereomer despite two stereocentres. Atom- and charge-balanced with explicit hydrogens.
cases.claim_boundary: Tests LOCALIZED multicentre behaviour of a second kind: two stereocentres are FORMED in one rewrite with a meso (internal-mirror) relationship, so the population is a SINGLE achiral stereoisomer, not a mixture. Infers no diastereoselectivity or reagent control; the meso outcome is the supplied oracle, not a prediction.

cases.name: 2-methylpyrrolidine N-acylation, ring centre retained

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Permutation correctness: the four references at map 2 (ring nitrogen 1, methyl 6, ring carbon 3, explicit hydrogen 12) are all immediate neighbours of the owner and none is permuted, replaced or removed by the rewrite, so the parity is necessarily preserved and classify_stereo_change(before, after) must return RETAINED. Reversibility: the reverse rule restores both the graph and the descriptor exactly.
cases.chemical_rationale: The ring bears exactly one stereocentre. C2 carries the ring nitrogen, a methyl, ring carbon C3 and one hydrogen; the two ring paths leaving C2 are constitutionally different (one reaches N directly, the other three CH2 groups), so C2 is genuinely stereogenic. Acylation forms the N-C bond and transfers the N-H proton (map 11) to chloride; no bond to C2 is made or broken. Every map on the left appears on the right with the same element.
cases.claim_boundary: Supplied endpoint descriptors are the oracle. The record asserts descriptor retention under a remote N-acylation only. It does not assert a rate, an acylation mechanism, a ring conformation, a preferred pucker, or any 3D geometry, and it does not claim the record is executable: SynKit must replay it.

cases.name: 3,3-dimethylcyclohexyl bromide thiolate substitution, inversion

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Typed virtual reference: @H:1 names its owning centre explicitly, so it survives any consistent relabeling of the heavy-atom maps together with its owner, and it is never confusable with a hydrogen owned by another centre. The owner is written [CH:1], which supplies exactly one implicit-hydrogen resource and carries no explicit hydrogen atom, so the reference resolves. Permutation correctness: the leaving ligand (map 7) and the incoming ligand (map 8) occupy the same descriptor slot while maps 2, 6 and @H:1 are retained unpermuted, so the parity flip encodes a genuine inversion rather than a relabeling artifact and classify_stereo_change must return INVERTED. Reversible: the reverse rule restores the graph and the original parity.
cases.chemical_rationale: The ring has exactly one stereocentre. C1 carries Br, one hydrogen and the two ring paths, which are constitutionally distinct because the gem-dimethyl quaternary carbon sits two bonds away on the C2 side and four bonds away on the C6 side; C3 itself is not stereogenic because it bears two identical methyls. Thiolate replaces bromide at a secondary ring carbon and bromide leaves as a mapped anion, so the map inventory and the -1 charge are identical on both sides.
cases.claim_boundary: The supplied before/after descriptors are the oracle. Inversion is a declared descriptor relationship, not a mechanistic claim: no backside-attack trajectory, transition state, ring conformation, anti-periplanar arrangement, rate or stereoselectivity is asserted. Not executable until SynKit replays it.

cases.name: 2-methylcyclohexan-1-ol O-acylation, two ring centres retained

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the two centres own independent registry keys (atom:1, atom:2), each descriptor referencing only immediate neighbours of its own owner, and each effect touches only its own key; the two centres are therefore checked independently rather than as one joint object. Permutation correctness: neither centre has a reference permuted or replaced, so both parities are preserved, and because the relative relationship is a function of the two parities it is preserved as well. Both centres must be validated separately: a rule that preserved only one of them would not satisfy this record. Reversible.
cases.chemical_rationale: The two ring stereocentres are non-equivalent: C1 carries the oxygen substituent, C2 carries a methyl, and their ligand paths differ, so no ring symmetry maps one onto the other. Acylation converts the O-H at map 7 into an O-acyl group and transfers the hydroxyl proton (map 13) to chloride; no bond to C1 or C2 is made or broken. The map inventory is identical on both sides and every map keeps its element.
cases.claim_boundary: The relative relationship between the two centres is a SUPPLIED endpoint oracle carried by the two relative descriptors. It does not encode a cis/trans label, does not prove a 3D conformation or an anti-periplanar geometry, and asserts nothing about diastereoselectivity or rate. Not executable until SynKit replays it.

cases.name: fleeting atom stereocentre in hex-2-ene to hex-1-ene isomerization

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Lifecycle correctness: step 1 FORMs the map-2 descriptor (before null, no reactant endpoint) and step 2 BREAKs the SAME relative descriptor (after null, no product endpoint); the after value of FORM and the before value of BREAK are the identical descriptor object, which is what makes the two steps compose. Because neither composed endpoint owns a map-2 descriptor, the composed change is FLEETING and is asserted ONLY in oracle.composed_stereo_changes; each elementary step declares the ordinary FORMED/BROKEN tokens, never FLEETING. Step chaining: the full product graph of step 1 is character-for-character the full reactant graph of step 2, including every explicit hydrogen. Reversibility applies to the elementary rules, reversing the sequence, not to a composed rule.
cases.chemical_rationale: Hydrogen chloride adds across hex-2-ene to give 2-chlorohexane, whose C2 carries four constitutionally distinct ligands (methyl, chlorine, the butyl-terminated chain and one hydrogen) and is therefore genuinely stereogenic. Elimination toward the terminal carbon then gives hex-1-ene. Both composed endpoints are alkenes in which map 2 is sp2 and owns no tetrahedral descriptor, so the net change is a constitutional alkene isomerization and the stereocentre exists only in the intermediate. Each step is element-, charge- and hydrogen-balanced, the transferred protons (maps 20 then 7) are explicit and mapped on both sides, and the map inventory is identical across each arrow.
cases.claim_boundary: This record asserts descriptor lifecycle only. It does not assert Markovnikov regiochemistry, an addition or elimination mechanism, a carbocation intermediate, thermodynamic preference between hex-1-ene and hex-2-ene, a rate, or reaction conditions. Not executable until SynKit replays both steps and their composition.

cases.name: fleeting planar bond in vicinal dehalogenation then dihalogenation

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Target ownership uses the smaller-larger bond key convention (bond:2-3). Lifecycle correctness: step 1 FORMs the 2-3 planar-bond descriptor (before null) and step 2 BREAKs the identical relative descriptor (after null), so neither composed endpoint owns a descriptor at that locus and the composed change is FLEETING, asserted only at the case-level oracle. ALL FOUR side references are heavy atoms: maps 1 (methyl carbon) and 6 (chlorine) are immediate neighbours of map 2, and maps 8 (chlorine) and 4 (methyl carbon) are immediate neighbours of map 3. No explicit hydrogen is referenced by any descriptor in this record, so no hydrogen reference can become stale or absent in a reverse reactant graph. Step chaining: the full product graph of step 1 is character-for-character the full reactant graph of step 2, which is why the zinc bromide and the iodine are carried explicitly through both steps as mapped spectators. Reversal is of the elementary sequence.
cases.chemical_rationale: Zinc-mediated vicinal dehalogenation of 2,3-dibromo-2,3-dichlorobutane gives 2,3-dichlorobut-2-ene, whose C2=C3 bond is genuinely stereogenic because each alkene carbon carries two different heavy substituents (a methyl and a chlorine). Addition of iodine across that alkene then gives 2,3-dichloro-2,3-diiodobutane. Both composed endpoints are sp3 vicinal dihalides that own no planar-bond descriptor at maps 2-3, so the locus exists only in the intermediate and the net change is a halogen exchange. Every step is element-, charge- and hydrogen-balanced, the map inventory is identical across each arrow, and every map keeps its element (5 and 7 are bromine throughout, 9 is zinc, 10 and 11 are iodine).
cases.claim_boundary: This record asserts descriptor lifecycle only. It does not assert an anti-periplanar elimination geometry, an E2 or E1cb pathway, anti addition of iodine, an iodonium intermediate, an E or Z preference for the intermediate, a rate, or reaction conditions. The intermediate's parity is 0 by class definition and encodes no geometry claim. Not executable until SynKit replays it.

cases.name: localized C2 epimerization of 3-aminobutan-2-ol, C3 retained

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership and LOCALIZATION: atom:2 and atom:3 are independent registry keys, each descriptor referencing only immediate neighbours of its own owner, and each effect touches exactly one key; this is what makes the behaviour localized rather than global. Strict matching gives ONE application: the substrate is asymmetric and the two centres are constitutionally distinguishable (map 2 carries oxygen, map 3 carries nitrogen), so no automorphism of the graph exchanges them and the descriptor guards select the map-2 locus deterministically; the rule cannot also fire at map 3. The rewrite is a connectivity identity, which is admissible here precisely because the strict descriptor guards make the forward product unique and the reverse product restores the original graph and stereo registry exactly: applying the rule twice returns the starting descriptor set. Permutation correctness: within each centre the four references are pairwise distinct, so the parity flip at map 2 is well defined and is not a relabeling artifact.
cases.chemical_rationale: 3-Aminobutan-2-ol has exactly two stereocentres and they are not equivalent: C2 carries a hydroxyl, a methyl, C3 and one hydrogen, while C3 carries an amino group, a methyl, C2 and one hydrogen. Because one centre bears oxygen and the other nitrogen, their ligand paths are constitutionally distinguishable and a change at one is unambiguously attributable. Epimerization inverts configuration at C2 alone while leaving C3 untouched; since epimerization changes no bond, the connectivity is identical on both sides and the entire change is carried by the descriptor registry. Explicit hydrogens throughout; the map inventory, element per map and charge are trivially identical.
cases.claim_boundary: The epimerization is a SUPPLIED descriptor-level oracle. No mechanism is asserted: no enolization, no deprotonation, no planar intermediate, no base, no conditions, no rate, and no thermodynamic preference between the two epimers. The record tests only that one locus inverts while its neighbour is independently verified as retained. Not executable until SynKit replays it, including the identity-rewrite determinism and reverse checks.

cases.name: 3-bromohexane hydrolysis with unspecified product orientation

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Outcome separation: the rule declares INFORMATION LOSS, not a population. The product descriptor keeps the tetrahedral class and a full complement of four references that are immediate neighbours of the owner, but its parity is null and its state is unknown, so classify_stereo_change(before, after) must return UNSPECIFIED. No rule_metadata.stereo_outcomes entry exists anywhere in this record, no branches are enumerated and no weights are given, so UNSPECIFIED can never collapse into RACEMIC or ENANTIOMERIC_MIXTURE. INTENTIONALLY NON-INVERTIBLE: reversal must FAIL with the documented non-reversible effect, because a null parity carries no information from which the original orientation could be reconstructed; a reverse application that returned a specified parity would be a bug this record is designed to catch.
cases.chemical_rationale: C3 of 3-bromohexane carries bromine, one hydrogen and two constitutionally different chains (ethyl on the map-2 side, propyl on the map-4 side), so it is genuinely stereogenic. Hydroxide replaces bromide, which leaves as a mapped anion, so the map inventory and the -1 charge are identical on both sides and every map keeps its element: map 7 is bromine throughout and map 8 is oxygen throughout.
cases.claim_boundary: The product orientation is UNSPECIFIED because the supplied rule does not determine it. This is an absence of information in the oracle, NOT a claim that the product is racemic, NOT a claim of any enantiomer ratio, and NOT a mechanistic claim about ionization, a planar carbocation, solvolysis or competing pathways. No rate, no selectivity. Executable after SynKit replay confirmed the forward result and deterministic reversal refusal.

cases.name: allylic chloride hydrolysis with unspecified alkene geometry

provenance.origin: synthetic software-conformance fixture authored for SynKit
provenance.generator: Claude (Anthropic) SynKit stereo final-8 candidate generator, v1

cases.mathematical_rationale: Relabeling invariance: the descriptor is defined by relative references to immediate neighbours of its owner, so any consistent map relabeling leaves it equivalent and leaves the rule result unchanged. Target ownership: the registry key is derived from the descriptor's own centre (or central bond) and matches every endpoint key and every effect key. Lifecycle correctness: the after descriptor has IDENTICAL class and all six IDENTICAL references to the before descriptor, differing only in parity (null) and state (unknown), which is the strictest reading of the unspecified contract and isolates the parity channel as the only thing under test; classify_stereo_change must return UNSPECIFIED. All four side references are heavy atoms: maps 1 and 8 are immediate neighbours of map 2, maps 9 and 4 are immediate neighbours of map 3, so no hydrogen reference exists to go stale. Outcome separation: no rule_metadata.stereo_outcomes entry exists, so the record cannot be read as an E/Z population; an unknown geometry and a mixture of geometries are different assertions and only the former is made. INTENTIONALLY NON-INVERTIBLE: reverse replay must FAIL with the documented non-reversible effect, and the fixture declares that expectation.
cases.chemical_rationale: The C2=C3 bond of 1-chloro-2,3-dimethylpent-2-ene is genuinely stereogenic because each alkene carbon carries two different heavy substituents: a chloromethyl group and a methyl on C2, a methyl and an ethyl on C3. The reacting bond is the allylic C1-Cl, one bond away from the alkene, so the four side references of the double bond are untouched by the substitution and only the orientation information is dropped. Chloride leaves as a mapped anion, so the map inventory, the element of every map and the -1 charge are identical on both sides.
cases.claim_boundary: The retained alkene geometry is UNSPECIFIED because the supplied rule does not determine it. This is information loss, NOT a claim of an E/Z mixture, NOT a ratio, and NOT a mechanistic claim about an allylic cation, SN1' or SN2' competition, or double-bond transposition. Not executable until SynKit replays it and confirms the reverse failure.

cases.name: SN1 product whose stereochemical composition was never measured

assertion.rationale: Same transformation as ST-01, but sourced from a record with no measured e.e. ST-01 may assert the idealized racemate because its rule_set_metadata declares the 0.5/0.5 population; this case may not. Emitting either C[C@H](O)c1ccccc1 or C[C@@H](O)c1ccccc1 as the product is a failure, and so is silently promoting the stereo-free SMILES to 'racemic'.

cases.name: Flexible E2 elimination without conformational information

assertion.rationale: 3-bromopentane is conformationally free, so anti-periplanar geometry does not fix the alkene descriptor. The E/Z locus must be marked underdetermined rather than defaulted to E on thermodynamic grounds. Contrast with DB-11, which supplies a rigid substrate and is therefore deterministic.

cases.name: Terminal alkene carries no E/Z element

assertion.rationale: C1 bears two hydrogens, so no E/Z element exists. Any directional bond written onto this double bond is meaningless and must not be reported as a descriptor.

cases.name: Alkene carbon with two identical substituents carries no E/Z element

assertion.rationale: C3 of 2-methylbut-2-ene bears two methyls. CIP ranking cannot distinguish them, so the double bond is not stereogenic. This is the duplicate-substituent case of NG-09; ST-43 is the duplicate-hydrogen case.

cases.name: Unreported product stereochemistry must stay unknown, not racemic

assertion.rationale: The graph edit is identical to ST-14/ST-15/ST-16. Those cases may assert racemic or a single enantiomer because their conditions declare the catalyst; this one may not. 'Unknown' and 'racemic' are different claims: the first says the composition was not determined, the second asserts a 50:50 measurement. Collapsing them destroys the distinction the catalog's Section 10 requires.

cases.name: Stereo-free SMILES needs external annotation to mean racemic

assertion.rationale: One and the same product SMILES backs both readings. The two annotations must round-trip to distinct records and must never compare equal. A serializer that writes the ensemble annotation into the SMILES, or a loader that drops it, fails this case. This is the annotation-layer counterpart of ST-45.

cases.name: Simple tertiary amine is not a stable nitrogen stereocentre

assertion.rationale: N-ethyldimethylamine inverts pyramidally far faster than it can be resolved, so the nitrogen must not be treated as a stereocentre even though it formally carries four different ligands counting the lone pair. Contrast HE-06, where quaternization removes the lone pair and creates a genuinely stable centre.

cases.name: Structurally impossible trans-cycloalkene must be rejected or flagged

assertion.rationale: trans-Cyclohexene is not isolable at ordinary conditions; the SMILES is structurally invalid rather than merely unusual. Verified behaviour of RDKit 2026.03.3: both C1/C=C/CCC1 and C1/C=C\CCC1 parse without error and canonicalize to C1=CCCCC1, i.e. the directional bonds are silently discarded rather than flagged. A conformance layer must therefore raise its own diagnostic; relying on the toolkit to reject the input is not sufficient.

catalog_backlog.reason: Needs a multi-centre epimerization substrate with an oracle that localises the single inverted centre; ST-21 covers the weaker 'react at one of two centres' claim.
A literal D-for-H replacement is not a single graph edit; encoding it needs the isotope-aware rewrite that ST-04/ST-06/ST-19 are blocked on.
Prochiral desymmetrization by mono-protection needs prochirality (pro-R/pro-S) annotation, which the schema does not yet carry.
Needs a reaction that maps an RR/SS pair onto a meso product while retaining both centres; no clean small-molecule instance chosen yet.

Net anti dihydroxylation via epoxidation then hydrolysis. Requires composing a face-rule pair (ST-25/ST-26) with an anti ring opening; the composed oracle is the target of a separate rule-composition test.
Halohydrin formation couples stereochemistry to regiochemistry; needs a regio oracle alongside the stereo oracle.
Hydroboration-oxidation of 1-methylcyclohexene is a ring case; blocked with the RG group below.
Trivially subsumed by ST-33 minus the priority reversal; worth adding as the control once ST-33 passes.
Rigid anti-periplanar E2 needs a conformationally locked substrate, i.e. a ring; blocked with RG.
C=N stereochemistry. RDKit assigns E/Z across C=N, but oxime/imine geometry needs its own oracle and lone-pair ranking checks.

Ring cases need cis/trans relative-stereochemistry annotation across a ring and, for RG-11..RG-16, carbohydrate and peptide substrates. None are encoded; fabricating them without an executable oracle would violate the catalog's own entry criterion.

Pericyclic selectivity is not a function of connectivity: conrotatory/disrotatory and endo/exo require an explicit thermal-vs-photochemical field and a facial descriptor the schema does not yet define.

Promoted native-descriptor rewrites now cover supplied atropisomer formation, inversion, preservation, and unequal branching. Allene and spiro extensions remain deferred and need an explicit representation before their catalog oracles can be trusted.

Promoted native-descriptor rewrites now execute representative S, P, Si, N+, sulfoxide-lone-pair, and phosphine-lone-pair behavior. These catalog IDs remain unassigned until their specific chemistry and catalog mapping are reviewed.
Pseudoasymmetric r/s centres exercise CIP Rules 4b/5; needs a dedicated oracle since a like/unlike ranking bug would silently pass every case above.
Partially covered by ST-04/ST-06/ST-19 for R/S; the isotope-dependent E/Z half has no case yet.
Enhanced stereo (ABS/AND/OR) groups. ST-46 encodes the racemic-vs-unknown distinction at the annotation layer, but real AND/OR group round-tripping needs an extended-SMILES or MOL V3000 path.

Supplied trigonal-bipyramidal and octahedral descriptors now execute through native graph rules. The catalog-specific optional coordination cases remain unassigned and no 3D geometry or stability is inferred.
Wittig and HWE default-geometry tests need ylide-stabilisation context fields; the assertion is about a missing input, so the case must carry the context schema first.
Partially covered by ST-18, which declares 'no cage model' in its conditions. A true NG-06 needs a positive cage-model counterpart to contrast against.

Section 11 asks for 14 serialization variants of every chemically distinct gold reaction. These are generated mechanically from the cases above rather than stored, except for variant 14 (equivalent-atom mapping permutation), which is encoded explicitly as ST-22 because it needs a hand-written second mapping.

non_tetrahedral_fixture_metadata.description: Four balanced mapped fixtures for forward/reverse non-tetrahedral and assigned-atrop rule application. These fixtures validate supplied graph stereochemistry, not reaction selectivity, barriers, 3D inference, or physical configurational stability.
non_tetrahedral_fixture_metadata.electronic_scope: Coordination cases use SynKit's declared scalar two-centre Lewis convention. Every endpoint passes the local formal-charge/resource audit. The octahedral cobalt center is explicitly Co+ because V=9, L=1, R=0, H=0, and six sigma bonds require q=+1.

provenance.representation_note: The ligand alphabets follow the non-tetrahedral representation families exercised by the RDKit Book and SynKit's pinned adapter fixtures; the transformations and expected outcomes are project-designed conformance cases.

negative_controls.expected_detection: strict stereo guard rejects the inverse/non-equivalent input orientation
descriptor topology validation localizes the non-adjacent owner/reference errors
strict stereo guard rejects an otherwise isomorphic host with an empty registry
the two assigned-atrop product branches remain exact-stereo non-isomorphic and survive ITS deduplication

candidate_promotion_metadata.selection_boundary: This promotion intentionally stops at 72 positive cases. Eight further independently reviewed positives are required before claiming an 80-positive stereo partition.

final_eight_promotion_metadata.selection_boundary: This promotion raises the public stereo partition to 80 positive cases. The two unknown/unspecified records are forward-executable and intentionally non-invertible; SynRule.reversed() refuses them deterministically instead of fabricating specified stereo.
